behavior_in_task_description.reason: Instruction names all four files, the 'non-draft' help text change, removal of LLM SDK usage, --to upper bound via GITHUB_SHA (implied by release point), UPCOMING_CHANGELOG in .gitignore, and AGENTS.md rules covering try/catch, single-word names, no any.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction describes what must change (deterministic commits, pin upper bound, no LLM SDK) without dictating specific code lines or exact function implementations.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests check behavioral properties (help contains 'non-draft', no SDK imports, --to flag, GITHUB_SHA reference, .gitignore entry) and general naming rules; multiple correct implementations would satisfy them.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests execute bun with --help, transpile via Bun.Transpiler subprocess, and run bun typecheck, in addition to structural checks for non-executable files.

test_not_tautological.reason: f2p tests require actual behavioral changes: --help must emit 'non-draft', transpiled output must omit @opencode-ai, version.ts must contain GITHUB_SHA and --to.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Each stated requirement (deterministic changelog, help text change, upper-bound pin, prompt change, .gitignore) has a corresponding test, plus AGENTS.md rule tests.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are grouped with section comment headers (Gates, Fail-to-pass pr_diff, Config-derived, Pass-to-pass) and each has a clear docstring.

structured_data_schema.reason: Task does not produce a structured data artifact with a schema contract.

no_typos.reason: File paths and commands (script/changelog.ts, .opencode/command/changelog.md, GITHUB_SHA, UPCOMING_CHANGELOG) are consistent across instruction, tests, and solution.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction names files to modify but does not cite specific line numbers or buggy expressions to replace.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: AGENTS.md rules are cited by line numbers; .gitignore edit is a single-line addition and the constraints are localized.
